I have a number of poorly formatted markdown files that contain character creation and rules information. The information is spread out across multiple markdown files. I have multiple goals:

1. Strip existing links from them leading back to D&D beyond and clean up the formatting to be cleanly formatted md while not losing any information/descriptions. This should leave us with cleaned up markdown files. 
2. Go through all of cleaned up files, and copy the following information into a new file:
- class information to a class specific named file.
- subclass information to a class specific named file. 
- species information into a species file. 
- feat information into a feat file.

Unfortunately, it is too much to handle in a single prompt. I can't lose ANY information from the source documentation. I'm trying to come up with an iterative plan and set of prompts to complete my goals without the agent being overloaded and context problems. What is the best way to do this?
